title: Engineering Mathematics_ Discrete Mathematics - Propositional Logic

questions.question:
Let \( \theta(x, y, z) \) be the statement “x+y=z” and let there be two quantification given as

  1. \( \forall x \forall y \exists z \theta (x,y,z) \)
  2. \( \exists z  \forall x \forall y \theta (x,y,z) \)

where x, y, z are real numbers, then which one of the following is correct?



UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 75
options: I is true and II is true
I is true and II is false
I is false and II is true
I is false and II is false
questions.correct_answer: I is true and II is false

questions.question:
Match the following :

\( \begin{array}{clcl}  & \textbf{List-I} & &\textbf{List-II} \\ \text{(a)} & \text{Vacuous} & \text{(i)} & \text{A proof that the implication p \rightarrow q is true based} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{on the fact that p is false.} \\ \text{(b)} & \text{Trivial} & \text{(ii)} & \text{A proof that the implication p \rightarrow q is true based} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{on the fact that q is true.} \\ \text{(c)} & \text{Direct} & \text{(iii)} & \text{A proof that the implication p \rightarrow q is true that proceeds} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{by showing that q must be true when p is true.} \\ \text{(d)} & \text{Indirect} & \text{(iv)} & \text{A proof that the implication p \rightarrow q is true that proceeds} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{by showing that p must be false when q is false.}  \\ \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Codes :} \)



UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 8

In propositional language \( P \leftrightarrow Q \) is equivalent to (where \( \sim \) denotes NOT)



UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 57
